name: MSC extracellular vesicles extended healthspan in mouse aging models
summary: MSC-derived extracellular vesicles reduced senescence markers and improved lifespan or healthspan readouts in mouse aging models.
statement: Young MSC conditioned media and extracellular vesicles rescued aged stem-cell and fibroblast phenotypes, extended lifespan in Ercc1-deficient mice, and reduced senescence or improved healthspan measures in naturally aged mouse experiments.
population_or_model: Cellular senescence systems plus Ercc1-deficient and naturally aged mouse models
time_horizon: Repeated preclinical treatment windows reported across cellular and mouse aging assays
quantitative_note: The staged baseline treats this as direct animal aging support, not as evidence of human functional benefit.
caveats: The strongest lifespan data came from a progeroid DNA-repair-deficient mouse model.
Mouse senescence and healthspan readouts do not establish durability, dosing, or safety for preventive human aging use.
The intervention class is heterogeneous; effects may depend on MSC source, donor age, culture conditions, vesicle isolation, and cargo.
